title: Warm Molten Jamaican Shrimp Pilaf
description: A spicy and flavorful Jamaican-inspired pilaf dish featuring succulent shrimp and aromatic spices, perfect for a post-workout meal. This dish is made with vialone nano rice and sashimi-cut shrimp for a unique texture and flavor combination.
instructions: Heat the coconut oil in a large saucepan over medium-high heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until translucent, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute, until fragrant.
Add the sashimi-cut shrimp and cook until pink and just cooked through, about 2-3 minutes per side.
Add the jamaican jerk seasoning, scotch bonnet peppers, and chicken broth to the saucepan, stirring to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10-12 minutes, or until the liquid has been absorbed and the rice is tender.
Stir in the chopped cilantro and lime juice.
Serve the pilaf hot, garnished with additional cilantro if desired.
